content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation PPP of length NNN. You are allowed to perform the following operation any number of times:


Choose any two numbers PiP_iPiвАЛ and PjP_jPjвАЛ such that the number of set bits (111s in their binary representation) is the same, and swap them.
Determine if it is possible to sort the permutation in ascending order using this operation. If possible, print Yes; otherwise, print No.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the length of the permutation PPP.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,P3,вА¶,PNP_1, P_2, P_3, \ldots, P_NP1вАЛ,P2вАЛ,P3вАЛ,вА¶,PNвАЛ, denoting the permutation PPP.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer вАФ `YES` it is possible to sort PPP in ascending order using this operation, and `NO` otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase. For example, the strings `YES`, `yeS`, `yes`, and `YeS` will all be treated as identical.
constraints: PPP is a permutation of {1,2,3,вА¶N}\{1, 2, 3, \dots N\}{1,2,3,вА¶N}
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5вЛЕ1055 \cdot 10^55вЛЕ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 222: : We can swap P1P_1P1вАЛ and P2P_2P2вАЛ because both have exactly one 111 in their binary representations.
[2,1вАЊ]вЖТi=1,j=2[1,2][\underline{2, 1}] \xrightarrow{i = 1,  j = 2} [1, 2][2,1вАЛ]i=1,j=2вАЛ[1,2].
Test case 333: : There is no possible way to sort PPP.
Test case 444: :

We can swap P2P_2P2вАЛ and P4P_4P4вАЛ because both have exactly one 111 in their binary representations
[4,1вАЊ,3,2вАЊ]вЖТi=2,j=4[4,2,3,1][4, \underline{1}, 3, \underline{2}] \xrightarrow{i = 2,  j = 4} [4, 2, 3, 1][4,1вАЛ,3,2вАЛ]i=2,j=4вАЛ[4,2,3,1].
We can swap P1P_1P1вАЛ and P4P_4P4вАЛ because both have exactly one 111 in their binary representations
[4вАЊ,2,3,1вАЊ]вЖТi=1,j=4[1,2,3,4][\underline{4}, 2, 3, \underline{1}] \xrightarrow{i = 1,  j = 4} [1, 2, 3, 4][4вАЛ,2,3,1вАЛ]i=1,j=4вАЛ[1,2,3,4].
